title: Scaled Dot-Product Self-Attention
description:
Implement single-head scaled dot-product self-attention for queries, keys, and values $Q, K, V \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times d}$ (sequence length $n$, head dim $d$):
$$\text{Attention}(Q,K,V) = \text{softmax}\!\left(\frac{QK^\top}{\sqrt{d}}\right)V$$

The softmax is applied over the key axis (the last axis of the $n \times n$ score matrix, i.e. row-wise), so each query's attention weights over all keys sum to 1. Use a numerically stable softmax (subtract the row max first).

Implement `self_attention(Q, K, V)` returning the attention output of shape `(n, d)`.

**Shapes**: `Q`, `K`, `V`: `(4, 8)` (seq_len=4, d=8) -> returns `(4, 8)`.
starter_code:
import numpy as np

def self_attention(Q, K, V):
    # TODO: softmax(Q @ K.T / sqrt(d), axis=-1) @ V
    pass
reference:
import numpy as np

def self_attention(Q, K, V):
    Q = np.asarray(Q, dtype=np.float64)
    K = np.asarray(K, dtype=np.float64)
    V = np.asarray(V, dtype=np.float64)
    d = Q.shape[-1]
    scores = (Q @ K.T) / np.sqrt(d)
    scores = scores - scores.max(axis=-1, keepdims=True)
    e = np.exp(scores)
    weights = e / e.sum(axis=-1, keepdims=True)
    return weights @ V
hints: Compute raw scores as Q @ K.T, scale by 1/sqrt(d) where d is the last dimension of Q/K, then apply a row-wise stable softmax over the key axis (axis=-1).
The attention output is the softmax weight matrix (n x n) times V (n x d): weights @ V.

solutions.name: Scaled dot-product attention
solutions.explanation: Each query's similarity to every key is measured by the dot product $QK^\top$, scaled by $1/\sqrt{d}$ to keep the softmax input variance roughly constant regardless of $d$ (since a dot product of $d$ independent unit-variance terms has variance $d$). Softmax over the key axis turns scores into a convex-combination weighting of $V$'s rows.
solutions.code:
import numpy as np
def self_attention(Q, K, V):
    Q = np.asarray(Q, dtype=np.float64)
    K = np.asarray(K, dtype=np.float64)
    V = np.asarray(V, dtype=np.float64)
    d = Q.shape[-1]
    scores = (Q @ K.T) / np.sqrt(d)
    scores = scores - scores.max(axis=-1, keepdims=True)
    e = np.exp(scores)
    weights = e / e.sum(axis=-1, keepdims=True)
    return weights @ V
